'Aura' by Izzy Rose Grange
2 layer hand-pulled screen print.42 x 59.4 cm on 300 gsm Munken Pure paper. Edition of 19, signed and numbered by the artist.

About the Piece
Aura is a print that captures the intersection of electricity and the mind, inspired by the elusive experience of migraine auras. While the exact causes of these auras remain a mystery, research suggests they may stem from electrical waves in the visual cortex.
 
These sensory disturbances, like dots, sparks, or zigzags in one's vision, often precede a migraine headache. 
In my personal encounters with migraine auras, I have witnessed a box-like shaped light disrupting my vision. 
This print translates my experiences into a visual representation using geometric shapes and rough textures. It invites viewers to explore the intricacies of perception and contemplate the electricity that is within us and to highlight the intricacies of the human condition. 

About the Artist

Izzy Rose Grange is a Dublin based artist and printmaker. She holds a BA in Fine Art Printmaking from the National College of Art and Design, Dublin, and has been creating work experimenting with integration and interaction with nature for a number of years.
Through a blend of images, geometric shapes, gradients, and pastel colours, Izzy invites viewers to contemplate the delicate balance between strength and vulnerability. Her prints often capture the power of the ocean as a symbol of human emotions and experiences. Artworks that inspire introspection and foster a connection to the natural world, her art serves as a testament to the beauty, fragility, and interconnectedness of our existence. 
Izzy is a tutor here at Damn Fine Print, teaching our Introduction to Risograph Workshop
Framing

Frames can be purchased through Damn Fine Print in black, white and natural wooden finishes, here. Bespoke framing is also available. Please note framed orders must be collected from the store and can't be shipped.
Shipping and ReturnsThis print is sold unframed securely packaged in a cardboard tube, sent via An Post Standard Postage. For details on shipping and returns click here. Please note colours may vary between computer screens.
objects.title: 'Aura' by Izzy Rose Grange

objects.description: Jack Winthrop's 20x20 fine art print edition titled "Virtuous Colors" is a captivating creation that celebrates the vibrant beauty of hues and the harmonious interplay of shades. In this captivating piece, Winthrop skillfully merges traditional symbolism with a contemporary flair, resulting in a composition that is both visually stunning and intellectually stimulating. The vivid colors, arranged in a captivating pattern against a backdrop exude an aura of energy and harmony. The meticulous attention to detail is evident in the precise brushwork, highlighting the artist's profound skill. The colors' radiance and their intermingling dance invite viewers to explore the depths of their own emotions and appreciate the inherent virtue found in embracing a vibrant and joyful existence. "Virtuous Colors" serves as a powerful reminder of the transformative power of vivid expressions and the pursuit of virtue in a world that craves vibrant positivity.
objects.title: "Virtuous Raven" 2020

objects.description:
Description:
The story of Wynwood Walls is one of change through passion, art and community. When Tony Goldman stumbled upon the expanse of stock warehouse buildings in Miami’s Wynwood neighborhood, he saw a blank canvas. In 2009, the celebrated visionary set out to transform the area into a center for cultural exploration—with the help of the world’s most innovative and recognized street artists. Walls of Change: The Story of The Wynwood Walls is a rediscovery of a decade of art, inspiration and innovation, with Wynwood’s most celebrated murals, featuring never-before-seen photography of The Walls’ development, and special commentary from street art’s most iconic figures, including Shepard Fairey, Maya Hayuk, Kenny Scharf, Ron English, and current curators Jessica Goldman Srebnick and the Goldman family, among others.
In just ten years, The Wynwood Walls has grown into a phenomenon in its own right, known as a milestone in artists’ careers , with an ability to catapult unknowns and veterans alike. Under the leadership of CEO Jessica Goldman Srebnick, The Wynwood Walls has become one of the highest profile street art destinations in the world, welcoming over three million visitors annually.
 
About the Authors:
Jessica Goldman Srebnick, CEO of Goldman Properties, is a second-generation leader of the company founded by her father, the late Tony Goldman—and since 2012, she has been the curator of The Wynwood Walls. In 2015, she cofounded Goldman Global Arts, a Miami- and New York-based creative collective. Jessica is an active member of the prestigious Young Presidents’ Organization, and she also serves as vice chair of the Town Square Neighborhood Development Corporation Board and co-chair of the Miami Super Bowl 2020 Host Committee. Since 2010, she has been a participant in the YPO/Harvard Business School President’s Program. In 2018, she was named one of Forbes’ Icons of Impact.
Hal Rubenstein is the author of six books—including the best-selling 100 Unforgettable Dresses—and is one of the founding editors of InStyle magazine, where he worked as fashion director for fifteen years. Hal has also served as style director of The New York Times Magazine, one of the founding editors of Details magazine, creator of the cult classic Egg magazine and a restaurant critic for both Details and New York Magazine. In 2019, the CFDA Lifetime Achievement Award winner launched the multiplatform The Happy Grownup, which celebrates the excitement, possibilities and relevance of life after fifty.
Martha Cooper is a Manhattan-based documentary photographer who has specialized in shooting urban vernacular art and architecture for over forty years, with her work appearing in a wide array of national and international publications. Martha’s books include Subway Art, a collaboration with Henry Chalfant; New York State of Mind; Tag Town; Tokyo Tattoo 1970 and Remembering 9/11. In 2009, Martha began photographing The Wynwood Walls while the first murals were being painted, and she has continued to document them every year since.
objects.title: Walls of Change: The Story of the Wynwood Walls - Special Plexi Edition
